latin: Nuntium telephonicum amico misi: 'amo te' scribere volui. machina autem verba mutavit in 'armo te.' amicus meus rogavit cur ei arma promitterem. haec machina me semper prodit.

questions.prompt: What did the narrator want to write?
What did autocorrect change the message to?

translation: I sent a phone message to my friend: I wanted to write 'I love you.' But the machine changed the words to 'I arm you.' My friend asked why I was promising him weapons. This machine always betrays me.
